principle: Tiny steps count. When activation is low, suggest the smallest safe next action and a recovery option.

categories.name: Hydration
actions: Put water beside you, even before drinking it.
Take one sip, then pause.
Add flavor/ice/straw if that reduces friction.
Set the cup on the path to the bathroom/bed/desk.

categories.name: Food / energy
actions: Pick a no-cook food first: crackers, yogurt, cheese, fruit, granola, leftovers, or a safe snack.
Eat three bites before deciding anything else.
Ask a caregiver/support person for one simple option, not a whole meal plan.
Use “fed is better than perfect” as the rule.

categories.name: Hygiene
actions: Choose the lowest-energy version: wipe face, deodorant, mouthwash, dry shampoo, clean shirt.
Sit down for hygiene whenever possible.
Use a shower chair, towel setup, and post-shower rest plan if showering.
Do one hygiene zone only: mouth, face, armpits, hair, or clothes.

categories.name: ADHD task start
actions: Name only the next visible action, not the whole task.
Set a 2-minute “touch the task” timer.
Open the tab/app/object, then stop if needed.
Use body-doubling or text someone “I am starting one tiny step.”

categories.name: Pain / mobility pacing
actions: Switch to seated, lying-down, or aided version.
Place needed items within reach before starting.
Break the task into reach-zone steps.
Budget recovery before and after, not only after.

categories.name: DBT emotion regulation
actions: Lower body arousal first: cold water/ice pack, slower exhale, feet on floor.
Use “name it to tame it”: feeling + intensity + trigger.
Choose one DBT skill before problem-solving.
Delay big messages or decisions until intensity drops.

categories.name: Connection / attachment
actions: Check facts before mind-reading.
Write the soft version of the ask: “Can you reassure me when you can?”
Use a 20-minute pause before sending many follow-ups.
Ask for one clear thing instead of testing the relationship.
